channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In polarized times, these Minnesota legislators want to restore civility at the state Capitol
item.description: This year’s legislative session started with a lot of vitriol between DFLers and Republicans. But a bipartisan group of legislators is trying to get both sides to be more collaborative and civil.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Trump pauses US military aid to Ukraine while pressuring Zelenskyy to move toward quick end to war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday directed a “pause” to U.S. assistance to Ukraine as he seeks to pressure Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to engage in negotiations to end the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE cuts all GSA employees across Nevada, senator says
item.description: President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) fired all General Services Administration employees across Nevada on Monday, according to Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why Trump's joint remarks to Congress won't be a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday night will stand at the front of the U.S. House chamber to address a joint session of Congress, the first of his second term in office.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: McMahon on Day 1 launches 'final mission' to send education back to the states
item.description: Secretary of Education Linda McMahon is informing all employees that the administration will embark on a "momentous final mission" to send education back to the states, Fox News Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Bill to ban trans athletes from girls' and women's sports teams fails to advance in Senate
item.description: A test vote on the bill failed to gain the 60 votes needed to advance in the chamber as senators stuck to party lines in a 51-45 vote tally.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuomo takes center stage in race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The race for New York City mayor is now officially revolving around former Gov. Andrew Cuomo following his declaration over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: WWE legend to lead Education Department after clinching final match in the Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon has been confirmed to serve as President Donald Trump's Secretary of Education after a full Senate vote on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon changes name of Georgia Army base back to Fort Benning, dumping Fort Moore
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has, for the second time, reversed the renaming of a U.S. military base, saying that Fort Moore in Georgia should revert back to being called Fort Benning.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Will Ukraine minerals deal happen after Zelenskyy fiasco? Trump to reveal in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters Monday that he would disclose where the deal stands when he addresses a joint session of Congress Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Trump's history with Russia and Ukraine set the stage for a blowup with Zelenskyy
item.description: As his White House meeting with his Ukrainian counterpart devolved into a stunning blowup, President Donald Trump leaned on a familiar refrain to explain his unique kinship with Russian leader Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Twin Cities meteorologist concerned about Trump cuts to NOAA
item.description: “I do worry about public safety,” Twin Cities meteorologist Paul Douglas told MPR News of the cuts to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. The concerns go beyond tornado warnings, he added. “It's the safety of our aviation system, shipping, agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Waltz likens Zelenskyy to an 'ex-girlfriend' after Oval Office spat
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz compared Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to an “ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: What you need to know about Trump’s address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Trump will lay out his second term agenda in an address to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday night. From talk on tariffs to the U.S. role in the world, here’s what to expect.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: How to watch the first joint address to Congress of Trump's second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday night will deliver the first joint congressional address of his second presidency. It's not officially called the State of the Union, a title reserved for a president's annual address to Congress during other years of an administration.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the U.S., British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as he prepared to host a summit of European leaders.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth directs civilian Pentagon workers to reply to OPM email
item.description: In a video posted to X by the DoD, Hegseth specified that only civilian workers should reply to the email and CC their "immediate supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: What U.S. lawmakers are saying about the White House clash between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Key Republicans and Democrats have been stalwart supporters of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. But a blowup between the Ukrainian leader and President Donald Trump is threatening to change that.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump makes U.S. copper mining a focus of his domestic minerals policy
item.description: It could mean a new day for U.S. copper mining, and new worries for environmental groups that are contesting proposals such as the stalled Twin Metals project near northern Minnesota's Boundary Waters, a lake-filled wilderness on the U.S.-Canada border.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada Democrats push for more ballot drop boxes to avoid election delays
item.description: Nevada Democrats unveiled a proposal Thursday to increase the number of ballot drop boxes in the days before an election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
